Name: Recognize bare tilde as path-like token
Description: Extend _is_path_like() in security_gate.py to return True for bare tilde (~) and tilde-prefixed paths (~/path). Currently ~ has no slash, backslash, .., or leading dot so _is_path_like returns False and commands like rm ~ pass without zone check. The shell expands ~ to HOME before execution. After _is_path_like returns True for ~, _validate_args will zone-check it -- since ~ resolves outside the project folder it will be denied. Write tests.
Goal: sanitize_terminal_command('rm ~', ws_root) returns deny. sanitize_terminal_command('rm ~/Documents', ws_root) returns deny. Tilde inside project folder paths still allowed.
Comments: Addresses BUG-048 from prior testing.

Name: Fix VIRTUAL_ENV bypass via python -m pip and startswith collision
Description: Fix two related VIRTUAL_ENV validation issues in security_gate.py _validate_args: (1) BUG-049: When pip is invoked as python -m pip install, the verb is python so the VIRTUAL_ENV guard (which is gated on verb in ("pip", "pip3")) never fires. Fix: in the -m module handler, add a VIRTUAL_ENV check when module is pip or pip3 and the subcommand is install. (2) BUG-050: The VIRTUAL_ENV check uses norm_venv.startswith(ws_root) which is a raw string prefix test. c:/workspace2/.venv matches c:/workspace. Fix: use norm_venv.startswith(ws_root.rstrip("/") + "/") or norm_venv == ws_root.rstrip("/"). Write tests for both.
Goal: python -m pip install requests without VIRTUAL_ENV returns deny. pip install with VIRTUAL_ENV=c:/workspace2/.venv and ws_root=c:/workspace returns deny. Both work correctly when VIRTUAL_ENV is inside the project folder.
Comments: Addresses BUG-049 (Critical) and BUG-050 (High) from prior testing.

When a .md file is read-only and contains placeholder tokens, the function should silently skip the write (consistent with the binary-safety guard on read). Write tests.
Goal: replace_template_placeholders does not raise PermissionError on read-only .md files containing placeholders. read-only files are silently skipped.
Comments: Addresses BUG-052 (Minor). Asymmetry between read-guard and write-guard.

Name: Add known SAE limitations to project copilot-instructions
Description: Update Default-Project/.github/instructions/copilot-instructions.md to add a short section documenting the 7 accepted SAE limitations and their workarounds. Keep it as compact as possible -- agents need to know WHAT is blocked and WHAT to use instead, nothing more. The 7 limitations: (1) Out-File blocked -> use Set-Content or > redirect, (2) Bare dir/ls/Get-ChildItem without path -> use list_dir tool, (3) Venv activation scripts -> use venv/Scripts/python.exe directly, (4) pip install via terminal -> use install_python_packages tool, (5) Direct venv python.exe execution -> use system Python, (6) Get-ChildItem -Recurse from project CWD -> use list_dir or file_search tool, (7) Memory tool -> blocked by design. Also sync templates/coding/.github/instructions/copilot-instructions.md to match. After changes run Default-Project/.github/hooks/scripts/update_hashes.py to re-embed hashes. Write tests.
Goal: Both copilot-instructions.md files contain the limitations section and are in sync. Hashes updated.
Comments: Documents accepted limitations L1-L4, L7-L9 from Security Audit V2.1.2.

Description: Fix two issues causing BUG-081: (1) Rewrite ts-python.cmd to use setlocal EnableDelayedExpansion and !VAR! syntax inside if ( ) blocks so that paths with spaces and special characters are expanded at execution-time not parse-time. (2) Rewrite verify_ts_python() in shim_config.py to bypass the fragile cmd.exe/batch chain: read python-path.txt directly via read_python_path() and test the Python exe via subprocess.run([str(python_path), '-c', 'import sys; print(sys.version)']) with stdin=DEVNULL and timeout=30. Fall back to the shim-based check only if direct verification passes (to confirm python works) and then verify the shim file exists at the expected location. This avoids cmd.exe /c entirely.
Goal: ts-python.cmd handles paths with spaces and special characters (including Program Files (x86)). verify_ts_python() succeeds on all Windows configurations without cmd.exe parsing errors. BUG-081 resolved.
Comments: BUG-081. Hotfix for v3.0.2.

Goal: Security gate tracks denials per session, shows progressive Block N of M messaging, and locks sessions at the configured threshold. All 7 ACs of US-034 (1-7) are satisfied.

Name: Add tool name normalization to security gate
Description: VS Code sends tool names in camelCase format (e.g. vscode_askQuestions) but the security gate checks against snake_case names (e.g. vscode_ask_questions). The check is case-sensitive exact-match set membership. This causes always-allow tools like vscode_askQuestions to be falsely denied. Add a normalization function that converts incoming tool names to the canonical format before checking against allowlists. Also add missing tool names that VS Code may send (get_terminal_output, view_image, terminal_last_command, terminal_selection, test_failure, tool_search, get_vscode_api, insert_edit_into_file). Update require-approval.sh and require-approval.ps1 with matching normalization. Run update_hashes.py after changes.
Goal: All VS Code built-in tools (including vscode_askQuestions and Plan Agent tools) pass through the security gate without false denials. Tool name comparison is case-insensitive or normalized. Hash integrity tests pass.

Name: Fix grep_search unfiltered NoAgentZone bypass
Description: Fix validate_grep_search() in security_gate.py so that grep_search calls without an includePattern cannot leak content from denied zones (NoAgentZone, .github/, .vscode/). Currently when no includePattern is provided, the gate returns allow and trusts VS Code search.exclude to filter results. search.exclude is not a hard security boundary. Fix: when grep_search has no includePattern, the security gate must either (a) deny the call and require an includePattern scoped to the project folder, or (b) inject a project-folder-scoped includePattern into the hook response to force VS Code to scope the search. Option (a) is simpler and fail-closed. The deny message should guide the agent to use includePattern with the project folder path. Update AGENT-RULES Known Workarounds if needed. Apply in templates/agent-workbench/. Run update_hashes.py. Write tests. Fixes BUG-172.
Goal: grep_search without includePattern returns deny from the security gate. grep_search with includePattern scoped to project folder continues to work. No NoAgentZone content is accessible via any search tool.
Comments: Implemented fail-closed fix in validate_grep_search(). Updated AGENT-RULES.md. Updated all tests affected by behavior change. All 17 SAF-066 tests pass.

Goal: orchestrator.agent.md CI/CD section has an explicit Human Approval Gate with: (1) draft creation, (2) wait for user verdict, (3) publish on approval or revision on rejection. No ambiguity remains.

Name: Golden-File Snapshot Infrastructure
Description: Document and operationalize the golden-file snapshot system in tests/snapshots/security_gate/. Create: (1) a README in tests/snapshots/ explaining the format, how to run, how to update. (2) A helper script or pytest conftest that auto-discovers snapshot files. (3) Update developer and tester agent files to reference the README for snapshot procedures. Fixes M-01/G-07.
Goal: tests/snapshots/README.md exists with run/update procedures. test_snapshots.py discovers and validates all snapshots. Developer and Tester agent files link to the README.

Name: Install vs Update Parity Testing
Description: Create automated parity verification: (1) CI workflow step in test.yml that creates a fresh workspace and an upgraded workspace, then diffs all security-critical files (must be byte-identical). (2) A local script (scripts/verify_parity.py) that does the same for manual pre-release checks. Uses MANIFEST.json to identify security-critical files. Ensures a user who downloads a fresh installer gets the exact same security files as a user who updates via the GUI.
Goal: CI test includes parity check job. scripts/verify_parity.py exists and returns exit 0 on parity, exit 1 on mismatch. Both verified by test run.

Name: Feature Behavior Locking via Snapshots
Description: Extend golden-file snapshot system to lock in intentional feature behavior. When a feature changes security_gate.py decisions (deny→allow or allow→deny), the snapshot test fails explicitly. The developer must update the snapshot AND document the change in dev-log.md as the new intended behavior. This prevents future agents from reverting the change because they find it 'undocumented'. The snapshot IS the documentation.
Goal: Snapshot tests fail on any security decision change. Snapshot update requires explicit --update flag. dev-log.md template includes a 'Behavior Changes' section for intentional decision changes.

Name: Document Regression Baseline Procedure
Description: Create documentation for tests/regression-baseline.json: format, when to update (after bug fixes, after intentional behavior changes), who updates (Developer after fix, Orchestrator at release). Add to testing-protocol.md and agent-workflow.md Mandatory Script Usage table if a script is needed. Fixes M-06.
Goal: testing-protocol.md contains a Regression Baseline section with format, update procedure, and ownership rules. All three agents (Developer, Tester, Orchestrator) know when it applies.

Name: Add generate_manifest.py to Mandatory Scripts
Description: Add scripts/generate_manifest.py to the Mandatory Script Usage table in agent-workflow.md. Document when it must be run (before any commit that modifies template files). Add a reminder to developer.agent.md pre-handoff checklist. Fixes M-03/ADR-04.
Goal: agent-workflow.md Mandatory Script Usage table includes generate_manifest.py with 'Developer' as user and 'Before committing template changes' as trigger.

Name: Populate ADR Related WPs
Description: Fill in the Related WPs column in docs/decisions/index.csv for all 6 ADRs. Cross-reference with workpackages.csv to find WPs that implemented each decision. Without this, the ADR index is unsearchable by WP. Fixes ADR-01/05.
Goal: All 6 ADR entries in index.csv have non-empty Related WPs columns. Each referenced WP ID exists in workpackages.csv.

Name: Document Branch Protection Requirements
Description: Create docs/work-rules/branch-protection.md documenting the required GitHub branch protection settings for main: require PR reviews, require test.yml checks to pass, no direct pushes. Reference from agent-workflow.md and commit-branch-rules.md. Note this requires manual GitHub repo settings.
Goal: docs/work-rules/branch-protection.md exists. Referenced from commit-branch-rules.md. Settings are documented step-by-step.

Name: Fix index.md Scripts Link
Description: Fix the display text for scripts/README.md in docs/work-rules/index.md. Current display text ../scripts/README.md is wrong — should be scripts/README.md or the correct relative path. Fixes M-05.
Goal: docs/work-rules/index.md link to scripts/README.md has correct display text and a valid target path. Click-through works.

Name: Clarify Restricted Zone Paths
Description: Fix ambiguity A-03 in agent-workflow.md Rules section. The sentence 'Never access .github/, .vscode/, or NoAgentZone/ in templates/agent-workbench/' is syntactically ambiguous. Rewrite to clearly separate repo-root restrictions (.github/, .vscode/) from template-relative restrictions (templates/agent-workbench/NoAgentZone/).
Goal: agent-workflow.md restricted zones are unambiguous. Each path is on its own line with clear scope (repo-root vs template-relative).

Name: Document Certification Pipeline Scope
Description: Add a note to generate_manifest.py --help and to MANIFEST.json that only templates/agent-workbench/ is manifested. templates/certification-pipeline/ is outside scope. Prevents confusion about why cert-pipeline files aren't in the manifest. Fixes missing documentation.
Goal: MANIFEST.json header or generate_manifest.py help text clarifies scope. No agent will expect cert-pipeline files in the manifest.

Name: Standardize Python Version in Examples
Description: Fix C-03: testing-protocol.md has Python 3.11 in CSV example but 3.13 in run commands. Standardize to the project's actual Python version throughout all example commands and CSV samples in testing-protocol.md.
Goal: All Python version references in testing-protocol.md use the same version. Grep for mixed versions returns 0.

Name: Fix update_architecture.py Exit Code
Description: Fix bug: update_architecture.py main() always returns 0 even on failure. finalize_wp.py checks returncode but the check is unreachable. Change main() to return 1 when the architecture file is missing or the pattern isn't found. Fixes skip point #9.
Goal: update_architecture.py returns exit code 1 on failure. finalize_wp.py correctly detects and warns on architecture sync failure. Verified by test.

Name: Fix MANIFEST CI Check Missing File
Description: Fix bug: test.yml and staging-test.yml manifest checks call sys.exit(0) when MANIFEST.json is missing — CI passes silently. Change to sys.exit(1) with a clear error message. Fixes skip point #10.
Goal: MANIFEST.json missing causes CI failure (exit 1) in both test.yml and staging-test.yml. Verified by test run without the file.

Name: Make --rc Flag Meaningful
Description: Fix A-07: release.py --rc is cosmetic only (prints a reminder banner). Either: (a) make it append -rc.N suffix to the version tag, or (b) remove the flag and document that all releases are draft by default. Choose the option that aligns with the draft→test→publish workflow.
Goal: --rc flag either creates a distinct RC tag or is removed. No cosmetic-only flags remain. release.py --help documents the behavior.

Name: Add Verified Bug Status
Description: Fix inconsistency: validate_workspace.py accepts 'Verified' as a valid bug status but update_bug_status.py does not allow setting it. Add 'Verified' to VALID_STATUSES in update_bug_status.py.
Goal: update_bug_status.py accepts --status Verified. VALID_STATUSES matches validate_workspace.py's VALID_BUG_STATUS.

Name: Extend Validation for Review Status
Description: Extend validate_workspace.py --wp to check artifacts for WPs in Review status, not just Done. A Review WP should have dev-log.md and test files. Currently only Done WPs are checked, so a WP reaches Review without artifacts and the validator doesn't catch it. Fixes skip point #8.
Goal: validate_workspace.py --wp checks dev-log.md and tests/ for Review WPs. A Review WP missing dev-log.md triggers ERROR.

Name: Fix Staging Test Version Check
Description: Fix: staging-test.yml only checks config.py and pyproject.toml for version consistency. Add setup.iss, build_dmg.sh, and build_appimage.sh to match release.yml's validate-version job which checks all 5.
Goal: staging-test.yml version check covers all 5 version files, matching release.yml validate-version.

Name: Create ADR-007 and migration plan
Description: Create ADR-007 documenting the CSV-to-JSONL migration decision. Update decisions/index.csv with ADR-007 entry. Create this migration plan with phased WP decomposition.
Goal: ADR-007 exists with full rationale. index.csv has ADR-007 row. Migration plan approved.
Comments: ADR-007 CSV-to-JSONL migration. Phase 1.

Name: Create jsonl_utils.py core library
Description: Create scripts/jsonl_utils.py as a drop-in replacement for csv_utils.py. Must provide identical public interface: FileLock, read_jsonl (replacing read_csv), write_jsonl (replacing write_csv), append_row, update_cell, locked_next_id_and_append, next_id, REPO_ROOT. JSONL format: one JSON object per line, UTF-8, no BOM. Atomic write-verify-rename. File locking reused from csv_utils.
Goal: jsonl_utils.py exists with all public functions. Unit tests pass for read/write/append/update/lock/next_id.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 1. Foundation for all subsequent migration WPs.

Name: Create CSV-to-JSONL conversion script
Description: Create scripts/migrate_csv_to_jsonl.py that converts all 7 CSV data files to JSONL format. For each CSV: read via csv_utils.read_csv, write via jsonl_utils.write_jsonl. Handle nested fields: convert comma-separated Depends On and Linked WPs into JSON arrays. Validate output row counts match input. Provide --dry-run flag. Delete original CSV files after successful conversion.
Goal: Running the script converts all 7 CSVs to JSONL with verified row count parity. --dry-run previews changes without writing.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 1. One-time migration tool.

Name: Migrate all data scripts to JSONL
Description: Update all 10 scripts that call csv_utils to use jsonl_utils instead: add_workpackage.py, add_bug.py, add_test_result.py, run_tests.py, finalize_wp.py, validate_workspace.py, dedup_test_ids.py, archive_test_results.py, update_bug_status.py, _add_wps_batch.py. Change all imports from csv_utils to jsonl_utils. Update all file path references from .csv to .jsonl. Update all function calls (read_csv->read_jsonl, write_csv->write_jsonl). Retire _repair_csvs.py and _verify.py (replace with JSONL equivalents or delete if no longer needed).
Goal: All 10 scripts use jsonl_utils. All path references point to .jsonl files. Scripts execute without errors.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 2. Largest single WP - touches 12 script files.

Name: Run CSV-to-JSONL data conversion
Description: Execute the migration script (MNT-016) to convert all 7 CSV data files to JSONL. Verify row counts match. Delete original CSV files. Commit the converted data files. This is the point of no return.
Goal: All 7 JSONL files exist with correct data. All 7 CSV files deleted. Git commit records the conversion.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 2. Data conversion execution.

Name: Update all agent definitions for JSONL
Description: Update all 6 agent files in .github/agents/ to reference .jsonl instead of .csv: developer.agent.md, tester.agent.md, orchestrator.agent.md, planner.agent.md, story-writer.agent.md, maintenance.agent.md. Update copilot-instructions.md (.github/instructions/). Change all path references, format descriptions, and operational instructions from CSV to JSONL.
Goal: All 7 agent/instruction files reference .jsonl paths. No CSV path references remain in any agent file.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 2. Agent definition updates.

Name: Update all work-rules docs for JSONL
Description: Update all 8 work-rules files that reference CSVs: index.md, agent-workflow.md, workpackage-rules.md, bug-tracking-rules.md, user-story-rules.md, testing-protocol.md, maintenance-protocol.md, recovery.md. Also update docs/architecture.md. Change all path references from .csv to .jsonl. Update format descriptions (columns -> fields, CSV schema -> JSONL schema). Update QUOTE_ALL references to JSON serialization. Update recovery procedures for JSONL.
Goal: All 9 documentation files reference .jsonl. No CSV format descriptions remain in operational docs.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 2. Documentation updates.

Name: Update all test files for JSONL
Description: Update ~25 test files across 14 WP directories that reference CSV operations: (1) Tests that read production CSVs (DOC-053, FIX-009, FIX-059, MNT-003) - update paths and readers, (2) Tests that test csv_utils (FIX-065) - rewrite for jsonl_utils, (3) Tests that mock csv_utils (FIX-059, FIX-066, FIX-068, FIX-081, FIX-082, FIX-098) - update mock targets, (4) Tests that assert CSV paths in docs (MNT-006, MNT-009, MNT-012, FIX-067, DOC-058) - update assertions to .jsonl. Run full test suite to verify.
Goal: All test files reference .jsonl. Full test suite passes with zero new failures from migration.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 3. Test infrastructure update.

Name: Retire csv_utils.py and verify clean state
Description: Delete scripts/csv_utils.py, scripts/_repair_csvs.py, scripts/_verify.py. Grep entire repository for any remaining .csv references (excluding historical maintenance logs and the ADR itself). Fix any found. Run validate_workspace.py and full test suite. Update regression baseline if needed. Final verification commit.
Goal: Zero .csv references in operational code/docs. csv_utils.py deleted. Full test suite green. Clean workspace validation.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 3. Final cleanup and verification.

Name: Update pre-commit hook for JSONL validation
Description: Update scripts/hooks/pre-commit and validate_workspace.py to validate JSONL structural integrity instead of CSV: each line must be valid JSON, required fields present, status enums valid, no duplicate IDs. Remove CSV-specific checks (QUOTE_ALL, overflow columns, bare newlines).
Goal: Pre-commit hook validates JSONL files. validate_workspace.py --full checks all 6 JSONL files for structural integrity.
Comments: ADR-007 Phase 3. Validation infrastructure update.

Name: Regenerate MANIFEST.json and fix pycache leak
Description: MANIFEST.json is stale: audit.jsonl hash changed during JSONL migration, and __pycache__/*.pyc files under templates/agent-workbench/ are detected as untracked by the manifest-check CI job. Fix: (1) add __pycache__ to .gitignore under templates, (2) delete the pycache directory, (3) regenerate MANIFEST.json via scripts/generate_manifest.py.
Goal: manifest-check CI job passes; no __pycache__ files under templates/

Name: Bulk-fix agent specification tests
Description: Update all ~452 DOC-category tests that assert old agent .agent.md frontmatter and content. The 10 Agent Workbench agents were redesigned (new personas, tool lists, descriptions) but ~452 tests still check the old values. Update each test to assert the current agent file content.
Goal: All DOC-category tests in tests/DOC-018/ through tests/DOC-058/ pass with correct assertions matching current .agent.md files
Comments: Wave 2 drift: agent redesign broke ~452 DOC test assertions. Largest single fix batch.

Name: Fix version-hardcoded test assertions
Description: Update all ~60 tests that hardcode obsolete launcher versions (3.2.x, 3.3.0-3.3.10) instead of using the dynamic version sourcing from tests/shared/version_utils.py. Convert each test to either use version_utils or assert the current version 3.3.11.
Goal: All tests referencing launcher version strings pass without hardcoded stale versions
Comments: Wave 4 drift: version churn from 3.2.x to 3.3.11 broke ~60 test assertions.

Description: Update all ~70 tests that reference the old template path templates/coding/ (now templates/agent-workbench/) and the old workspace prefix TS-SAE- (now SAE-). Includes ~40 template-path tests and ~27 GUI prefix tests.

Comments: Wave 1+3 drift: template rename and prefix rename broke ~70 test assertions.

Name: Fix CI, codesign, and security test assertions
Description: Update all ~200 tests in CI/CD (INS-013 through INS-017), codesign (FIX-028 through FIX-039), and security (SAF-060+) test directories whose assertions are stale due to workflow changes, macOS runner updates, signing approach changes, or security gate evolution.
Goal: All INS, SAF, and codesign-related FIX tests pass with current CI workflow and security gate behavior
Comments: Mixed drift across CI runner changes, codesign approach, and security gate updates. ~200 tests.

Name: Fix JSONL migration test assertions
Description: Update all ~30 tests in MNT-014 through MNT-023 test directories whose assertions reference old CSV file formats, csv_utils imports, or pre-migration data structures. Tests should verify current JSONL-based data files and jsonl_utils usage.
Goal: All MNT-category tests for JSONL migration workpackages pass with current JSONL file formats
Comments: Wave 5 drift: JSONL migration (ADR-007) changed data file formats but ~30 tests still assert CSV.

Name: Reset regression baseline and verify CI green
Description: After all FIX-103 through FIX-107 are complete: reset tests/regression-baseline.json to zero known failures (_count: 0, empty entries). Run the full test suite locally on Windows. Verify the CI test gate (run-tests job) passes on all 3 platforms by triggering test.yml.
Goal: tests/regression-baseline.json has _count: 0 and CI run-tests passes on Windows, macOS, and Linux
Comments: Final verification step. Must run after all test fixes are merged.

Name: Update testing protocol and propose ADR-008
Description: Update docs/work-rules/testing-protocol.md to add a mandatory rule: any code refactor that changes externally-asserted paths, identifiers, versions, formats, or agent content MUST update affected tests in the same PR. Create ADR-008 documenting the tests-track-code process rule and the root cause of the CI failure wave.
Goal: testing-protocol.md contains the new refactor-tests rule and ADR-008 is created in docs/decisions/
Comments: Process guard to prevent recurrence of the stale-test drift that blocked releases.
